objective: Prove explicit opt-in Quest particle adapters against the accepted Matter/Lattice/Optics contracts in the Spatial Camera Panel and native renderer second consumer, with parity, inert-denial, and rollback evidence.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the reusable particle adapter and device validation boundary.
Document explicit particle adapter activation and rollback.
Document the independent consumer and shared contract.
Record the landed Quest particle adapter routing.
Record the two-device particle adapter validation route and receipts.
Record cross-consumer promotion and rollback evidence.
Record the adapter and second-consumer impact map.

non_scope: Changing Matter simulation semantics or accepted schema fields.
Sharing private shaders, effect policy, study defaults, assets, or product tuning.
Enabling camera, tracked-hand, stereo-video, staged-asset, virtual-room, remote-peer-media, or unrelated feature families.
Treating screenshots or raw ADB readback as sufficient acceptance without runtime receipts.

acceptance.proof: Only an explicit particle adapter descriptor/profile can activate the reusable path; denied and absent features remain inert.
CPU oracle and Quest adapter outputs preserve identity, count, bounds, and bounded numerical tolerance.
Spatial Camera Panel and native renderer consume the same contracts through app-local composition without shared product policy.
The prior app-local or disabled profile remains selectable and passes its existing tests.
When visual/runtime promotion is claimed, both connected Quest devices emit effective-feature and render evidence with cleanup.

validation.command: focused Rust/Kotlin/Java adapter and static feature-lock tests
Rusty Quest tools/check_all.ps1 plus both app build/test profiles

outputs: Reusable Quest particle adapter with no app policy.
Explicit descriptors for origin and second consumer.
Host parity, app build, device scorecard, and rollback receipts.
Updated AGENTS, README, and skill routers.
commit_policy: Commit focused adapter/tests first, then app adoption and receipts; push source before planning at particle promotion.
